article.title: Kalshi says it will block politicians and athletes from trading in markets they’re tied to
article.summary:
The guardrails we built use state-of-the-art technology and screening lists, but no screening system is perfect, and motivated bad actors consistently try to find a way.
To that end, we are also adding a whistleblower functionality straight in our market page, which makes it easier for our community to flag potential violations as they go through our public trading data.

article.title: BlackRock's Larry Fink: Invest in AI or get left behind
article.summary:
The CEO of the world's largest asset manager unsurprisingly wants more people to invest in capital markets.
But he's also clear about the risk for those who do not or cannot invest in things such as the growth of artificial intelligence.
AI "threatens to repeat that pattern at an even larger scale —concentrating wealth among the companies and investors positioned to capture it," he added.
The "civic miracle" of being a part of your country's growth via investment is an important connection between citizens and their government, Fink wrote.
"Extending it — so that more people can invest in their country's growth and share in its rewards — is the task in front of us," his letter reads.

article.title: Trump says 'good' talks ongoing with Iran but offers few details
article.summary: If talks did take place, it is not clear how serious any US-Iranian negotiations have been, whether the US and Israel will continue other airstrikes and whether Iran is still restricting access to the Strait of Hormuz - the reason for Trump's Monday bombing deadline in the first place.

article.title: Oil above $100 over conflicting claims on US-Iran talks
article.summary:
Wael Sawan, chief executive of Shell, told an energy industry conference in Houston: "South Asia was first to get that brunt.
That's moved to south east Asia, north east Asia and then more so into Europe as we get into April."

article.title: Zalos raises $3.6M to automate finance workflows
article.summary:
The YC Fall 2025 startup, founded by a former Agicap GM and a former Apple Pay engineer, converts screen recordings of finance workflows directly into computer agents, no API integration required.
Enterprise finance teams typically run on a combination of ERPs, CRMs, spreadsheets, email, and banking platforms that were built at different times, by different vendors, for different purposes.
Rather than requiring API integrations or custom connectors, Zalos trains agents from screen recordings of the actual workflows finance teams run inside their existing tools.
Zalos is making a different bet: that finance operations require accuracy levels, audit trails, and domain-specific skills (Excel manipulation, ERP navigation, categorisation logic) that general-purpose agents cannot reliably provide.
The company’s current customers are in midmarket and enterprise finance teams; it plans to expand into additional enterprise ERPs and on-premise systems with the new capital.

article.title: Business Insider welcomes Ryan Kailath as senior editor on the economy team
article.summary:
Ryan Kailath joins Business Insider as a Senior Editor on our Economy team and will oversee a team of reporters covering the job market, student loans, and the intersection of culture and the economy.
Ryan has more than a decade of experience covering the economy, business, and the financial forces shaping everyday life.
As an editor at KUOW, he worked across multiple beats and contributed to award-winning stories recognized with Edward R. Murrow Awards.
"Ryan's combination of technical and business chops is the perfect complement to our people-focused economy coverage," said Bartie Scott, deputy editor at Business Insider.
He was a Knight-Bagehot Fellow in Business and Economics Journalism, focused on economics, accounting, and statistics, business analytics, and corporate finance.

article.title: Swimply's CEO says the app is bringing the gig economy to pools — and helping cash-strapped homeowners make money
article.summary:
The gig economy is coming for your backyard pool.
The app lets owners list their pools, where potential users can then book them by the hour.
"Now, they have a more epic backyard, and start charging more and gaining more traction for their pool," Laskin said.
Swimply still relies mostly on word of mouth to recruit new pool owners to its platform, Laskin said.
There's also potential to add more types of facilities to Swimply, Laskin said, from basketball courts to music studios.

article.title: Reddit CEO Steve Huffman says he plans to 'go heavy' on hiring new grads because they are 'AI native'
article.summary:
Reddit CEO Steve Huffman, on the other hand, says the opposite is true for his company.
Also, the best new grads, if you don't hire them as new grads, you will never see them," Huffman said.
"They will never be on the job market again.
Despite his optimism, new college graduates are entering the job market at a tricky time.
Still, Huffman says he'll keep hiring new grads, particularly in engineering.

article.title: Worried about a shaky stock market? This is what financial advisers suggest you do
article.summary:
Worried about a shaky stock market?
Stock markets have gone in the opposite direction, driven by uncertainty about the war's future and its effects on the economy.
Financial advisers say it depends on when you need to tap into your funds.
That's not to say a long period with a weak stock market is impossible.
It's classic advice to have a spread of holdings from the start, but when you are younger, those can be weighted toward the highly rewarding but fickle stock market.

article.title: China pledges more balanced trade and further opening of the economy after record surplus
article.summary:
How significant is China's record trade surplus impact?
China will import more high-quality foreign goods and work with all ‌parties to promote optimised and balanced trade development and expand the global trade pie, Li told the China Development Forum in Beijing, according to state media.
It comes after the world's second-biggest economy reported a record $1.2 trillion trade surplus for 2025.
In a separate speech at the forum, China's central bank governor Pan Gongsheng also sought to alleviate concerns surrounding the trade surplus.
FOREIGN INVESTMENT PUSHChina is working to reverse a decline in foreign direct investment, which fell 5.7% year-on-year to just over 92 billion yuan ($13.36 billion) in January, following a 9.5% drop over the course of 2025.

article.title: The $50,000 EV Scam: Why Washington Is Blocking the Affordable Chinese Cars Americans Actually Want
article.summary:
Tariffs exceeding 100% block affordable Chinese EVs as frustrated consumers pay $50,000 for average new carsGrew up back East, got sick of the cold and headed West.
Meanwhile, Chinese EVs dominate global markets with premium interiors, advanced driver assistance, and yes—actual mini-fridges.
Edmunds editor Clint Simone called Chinese EV tech “astounding” for the price after CES testing, highlighting karaoke systems and sophisticated driving software in sub-$25,000 vehicles.
Politics Versus PocketbooksOver 100% tariffs protect American jobs and data security while frustrating cost-conscious consumers.
The Tariff WorkaroundGeely plots American entry through local manufacturing that could reshape the affordable EV landscape.

article.title: The US government just banned consumer routers made outside the US
article.summary:
In December, the Federal Communications Commission banned all future drones made in foreign countries from being imported into the United States, unless or until their maker gets an exemption.
But since the vast majority — if not all — consumer routers are manufactured outside the United States, the vast majority of future consumer routers are now banned.
By adding all foreign-made consumer routers to its Covered List, the FCC is saying it will no longer authorize their radios, which de facto bans new devices from import into the country.
In the Volt Typhoon hack, Chinese state-sponsored hackers primarily targeted Cisco and Netgear routers, routers designed by US companies, according to the Department of Justice.
While the FCC’s Covered List makes it sound like the US is banning all “routers produced in a foreign country,” it’s defined a bit more narrowly than that.

article.title: Kalshi says it will crack down on politicians and athletes betting
article.summary:
As lawmakers zero in on insider trading risks, Kalshi said it is rolling out a fresh set of guardrails for its sports and political betting markets.
The move was made to comply with congressional proposals that prohibit insider trading, the company said.
Additionally, people in college and professional sports, including athletes, personnel, and referees, will be "preemptively blocked from trading markets associated with sports they are involved in."
Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour responded to Ocasio-Cortez's X post, saying that Kalshi "already bans, monitors, and enforces against all the groups" that she had mentioned.
While Kalshi has been publicly cracking down on users trying to profit from nonpublic information, its rival Polymarket is going the other way.

article.title: OpenAI's talent pipeline: Who's feeding and hiring away workers at Sam Altman's AI giant
article.summary:
Since it launched ChatGPT, the Sam Altman-led company has quickly become a magnet for AI talent.
Live Data Technologies analyzed publicly available professional profile data for OpenAI employees who had available information on previous employers.
The battle for AI talent has become one of Silicon Valley's fiercest.
It was founded by former OpenAI researchers, including siblings Dario and Daniela Amodei.
The average tenure for US-based OpenAI employees is around 16 months.

article.title: Tech Leaders Support California Bill to Stop 'Dominant Platforms' From Blocking Competition
article.summary:
Manipulating the order of search results to favor a provider's products or services, irrespective of a merit-based process,Using non-public data generated by third-party sellers — including sales volumes, pricing, and customer behavior — to develop competing products that are subsequently boosted above the third-party sellers' product...A new bill proposed in California "goes after big tech companies" writes Semafor .
Supported by Y Combinator, Cory Doctorow , and the nonprofit advocacy group Fight for the Future, it's called the "BASED" act — an acronym which stands for "Blocking Anticompetitive Self-preferencing by Entrenched Dominant platforms.
"More from Scott Wiener;s announcement The announcement includes a quote from Teri Olle, VP of the nonprofit Economic Security California Action, saying the act would "safeguard merit-based market competition.
This legislation stands for a simple principle: owning the stadium doesn't mean that you get to rig the game."
Read on for reactions from DuckDuckGo, Proton, Yelp, Y Combinator, and Cory Doctorow.

article.title: NeuReality appoints ex-Google AI director as adviser
article.summary:
On 12 March, NeuReality unveiled NR-NEXUS, describing it as a hardware-agnostic operating system for what the company calls AI factories.
Beta customers are already running the software, according to the company, though NeuReality has not disclosed which organisations are in the programme.
Deloitte estimates that inference workloads accounted for half of all AI compute in 2025 and will reach two-thirds this year.
Hyperscalers are responding with enormous capital expenditure, with Amazon projecting $200 billion in 2026 spending and Google budgeting between $175 billion and $185 billion, according to recent earnings disclosures.
For NeuReality, the appointment of an adviser with Google-grade product instincts may be a modest move on paper.

article.title: Apple’s Worldwide Developers Conference returns the week of June 8
article.summary:
March 23, 2026PRESS RELEASEApple’s Worldwide Developers Conference returns the week of June 8Available online around the world, the conference will showcase the latest Apple software and technologiesCUPERTINO, CALIFORNIA Apple today announced it will host its annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) online from June 8-12, bringing developers together from around the world for a week of connection, exploration, and innovation.
The conference will take place on the Apple Developer app, website, and YouTube channel; and on the Apple Developer bilibili channel in China.
Space will be limited; details on how to submit a request to attend can be found on the Apple Developer website.
Apple will share additional conference information in advance of WWDC through the Apple Developer app, website, and YouTube channel.
Developers can also follow the conference worldwide on LinkedIn and WeChat, and on bilibili in China.

article.title: Storing Solar Energy As Ice For Air Conditioning
article.summary:
Thermal energy storage is pretty great, as phase-change energy storage is very consistent with its energy output over time, unlike chemical batteries.
PV solar power charges a battery until it’s fully charged.
The process can later be reversed, which will draw thermal energy out of the surrounding air and thus provide cooling.
After charging the main compressor loop with R600 (N-butane), the system is trialed with a small PV solar array that manages to freeze the entire bucket of water.
Courtesy of insulation, it’s kept that way for a few days, giving plenty of time for the separate glycol-filled loop to dump thermal energy into it and push cold air into the surrounding environment.

article.title: UN issues new climate warning as El Niño looms
article.summary: While scientists are still working out exactly why the Earth has accumulated so much extra heat over the past decade or so, they have no doubt that heat-trapping greenhouse gases such as carbon dioxide (CO2) are the root cause of the imbalance.

article.summary: The Labour government has banned new licences for new oil and gas fields in the North Sea and a government spokesperson said issuing exploration permits "cannot give us energy security and will not take a penny off bills".

article.title: Microplastics are falling from the sky and polluting forests
article.summary:
Instead, most microplastics arrive through the air and gradually build up in forest soils.
"Then, in deciduous forests, the particles are transported to the forest soil by rain or the autumn leaf fall, for example."
Measuring Microplastics in Soil, Leaves, and AirTo better understand how microplastics accumulate, the research team collected samples from four forest sites east of Darmstadt in Germany.
This helped them assess how much of the total pollution stored in forest soils can be traced back to airborne sources.
Forests as Indicators of Airborne Plastic Pollution"Our results indicate that microplastics in forest soils originate primarily from atmospheric deposition and from leaves falling to the ground, known as litterfall.

article.title: Musk Offers Sneak Peek at Orbiting Data Centers. They're Bigger Than the ISS
article.summary:
Elon Musk offered a first look at his plans for orbiting data centers this weekend, and they will be longer than the International Space Station (ISS).
On the Earth’s surface, companies, including Musk’s xAI, are already planning terrestrial AI data centers that’ll reach 1,000 megawatts (1 gigawatt) of computing capacity.
Astronomers and environmentalists have also been raising alarms about light pollution from the proposed orbiting data centers.
(Credit: SpaceX)“It’s a hostile environment in space, so you want to design the chip, optimize it, for space,” Musk said.
Nevertheless, Musk remains bullish on orbiting data centers, pointing to the plentiful solar energy the satellites can harness.

Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf also warned on X that critical infrastructure and energy facilities across the Middle East could be "irreversibly destroyed" if Iranian power plants were attacked.

article.title: The world just lived through the 11 hottest years on record — what now?
article.summary:
The past 11 years (2015-2025) have been the hottest on record, with last year being the second or third warmest year since observations began, according to a report released today by the World Meteorological Organization (WMO).
The State of the Global Climate 2025 report, which tracks important climate indicators, found that atmospheric carbon dioxide concentrations and ocean temperatures reached record levels last year.
Global surface temperatures were slightly lower in 2025 than the previous year — the hottest on record — but continued a run of exceptionally high temperatures, the report states.
Last year, the EEI reached its highest level since observations started in 1960, the report states.
The concentration of two more greenhouse gases, methane and nitrous oxide, also reached the highest levels on record in 2024.

article.title: Most people get food’s environmental impact completely wrong, study finds
article.summary:
A new study offers fresh insight into how people judge the environmental impact of the foods they eat, and the results suggest many are getting it wrong.
Scientists measure a food's environmental impact using a life cycle assessment, which tracks the entire process from production to disposal.
Many participants overestimated the environmental impact of processed foods while underestimating the impact of water-intensive products (e.g.
They were also surprised to learn how much higher the environmental impact of beef is compared to other meats like chicken.
Environmental impact labels that give foods a single overall grade (such as A-E) could help make these comparisons easier for consumers."

article.title: Beavers are turning rivers into powerful carbon sinks
article.summary:
Beavers may play an unexpected role in tackling climate change by transforming rivers into effective carbon dioxide sinks, according to a new international study led by researchers at the University of Birmingham.
The results show that wetlands shaped by beavers can store carbon at rates up to ten times higher than similar areas without their presence.
By slowing water, trapping sediments, and expanding wetlands, they turn streams into powerful carbon sinks.
Beaver dams reshape rivers and carbon storageBeavers are returning to rivers and natural habitats across Europe after years of conservation efforts.
These carbon reserves can remain in place for decades, indicating that beaver-modified wetlands can serve as stable, long-term carbon sinks as long as the dams remain intact.

article.title: Nebraska Wants To Build Batteries For Storing Renewable Energy
article.summary:
That's why a lot of solar energy setups introduce a backup battery or a whole-home backup battery system.
The battery is charged, where the energy is stored, and then it uses that energy at times when there's little or no energy being generated, such as at night.
If the sun isn't shining, the system isn't producing energy, and more broadly, if the wind isn't blowing, wind generators aren't making energy either.
That's precisely why Nebraska has introduced a bill to significantly bolster the state's backup battery systems.
The goal is to generate energy from traditional fossil fuels and renewable sources like wind and solar, store it appropriately, and leverage it when energy demand rises.

article.summary:
Markwayne Mullin confirmed as the next secretary of Homeland Securitytoggle caption Manuel Balce Ceneta/APThe Senate confirmed Sen. Markwayne Mullin on Monday to serve as the next secretary of Homeland Security, putting the Oklahoma Republican in charge of immigration enforcement, one of President Trump's biggest priorities in his second term.
He will be the second secretary to lead the department during this Trump administration, replacing Kristi Noem.
I am scared of failure, and so I will work hard each day," Mullin said during the hearing.
Outgoing Secretary Noem was the face of many of these efforts, frequently appearing on television and in paid advertisements, or offering statements that defended the administration's actions.
With Trump and his advisers in the White House directing strategy from the top, it is unclear how much Mullin will change the dynamic.

article.title: Long TSA wait times persist despite ICE deployment as a potential deal to end the shutdown takes shape: Latest updates
article.summary:
The Transportation Security Administration has been critically short-staffed for more than a week as thousands of its security officers, who have been working without pay since the Department of Homeland Security’s funding lapsed five weeks ago, have been calling out of work every day.
President Trump announced on Saturday that he would reassign agents from Immigration and Customs Enforcement to support the TSA.
As of Tuesday evening, it was unclear how much of an impact ICE’s presence was having.
Wait times at Houston’s George Bush Intercontinental Airport reached four hours as the airport closed all but two of its security checkpoints.
In Atlanta, where waits reportedly stretched to as long as six hours over the weekend, airport management advised travelers to allow at least four extra hours to account for delays at security.

article.title: Survey Shows Few Biden Migrants Were Asylum Seekers
article.summary:
A survey by pro-migration groups shows that only one out of every 27 border migrants claimed to meet the core requirement for winning political asylum in the United States.
The data undermine routine claims by progressives and journalists that illegal migrants are seeking deserved asylum.
Congress’s immigration laws bar economic migrants, and the asylum exception is granted to migrants fleeing persecution by their home governments.
Several thousand migrants died while trying to reach the welcome offered by Biden’s deputies, including his pro-migration border chief, Alejandro Mayorkas.
Some migrants self-deported from the United States as ICE implemented the President’s deportation promise and mandate.

article.summary: He said he met Peter Wai at a restaurant in Chinatown, and was told that he was a police officer before later finding out he also ran a private security company - and so asked him to help with security for HKETO.

article.title: BBC in Cuba as island plunged into darkness
article.summary:
More than 10 million people in Cuba have had power cut to their homes and businesses after the country's national electrical grid collapsed.
The Caribbean island has been hit with several blackouts this month, as a result of a US fuel blockade that cuts off the foreign oil imports needed to keep power stations running.
The Communist-run country has an ageing electricity infrastructure and chronic fuel shortages.
The BBC's Will Grant reports from the capital Havana.

article.title: German president calls Iran war a disastrous mistake, in rare rebuke of Trump
article.summary:
By Andreas Rinke and Miranda MurrayBERLIN, March 24 (Reuters) - The Iran war is a "disastrous mistake" that breaches international law, Germany's president said on Tuesday in an unusually blunt ‌rebuke of U.S. President Donald Trump's foreign policy, which he said marked a rupture for ‌German ties with its biggest post-war ally.
Advertisement Advertisement"Our foreign policy does not become more convincing just because we do not call a breach of international law a breach of international law," Steinmeier, a former foreign minister from the ‌centre-left Social Democratic Party, said in ⁠a speech at the foreign ministry.
For, in my view, this war is contrary to international ⁠law," he said, adding he had little doubt that the justification of the imminent nature of an attack on U.S. targets did not hold water.
Calling the war unnecessary and a "politically disastrous mistake", Steinmeier said Trump's second ​term ​marked a rupture in German foreign relations as profound ​as Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
(Reporting by Andreas Rinke and Miranda MurrayWriting by Miranda Murray and Madeline ​Chambers Editing by Ludwig Burger and Aidan Lewis)

article.title: Tehran residents express distrust over Trump’s war diplomacy
article.summary:
“If we trust him, we lose.”People in Tehran have reacted with scepticism to Donald Trump’s comments on “negotiations” with Iran, after he announced the US was pausing strikes on Iranian energy sites.
Iran’s Foreign Ministry has denied the two sides are talking.

article.title: Can the Iran war even be won?
article.summary:
In tactical terms, Iran is the obvious loser of this war: Its senior leadership has been assassinated, and its military assets decimated, with minimal casualties on either the US or Israeli side so far.
If the US backs down under economic coercion, Iran will have just proven its ability to hold the global economy hostage and get away with it.
That means it would end the conflict in a stronger political position than where it started, despite its overwhelming battlefield defeats — which is what victory looks like.
This would represent a catastrophic failure of planning and foresight by the US-Israel coalition, almost certainly traceable back to Trump himself.
The president launched the war with no obvious endgame and has constantly shifted objectives, thus proving that all the tactical might in the world cannot make up for a complete lack of strategic direction.

article.title: Pakistan ‘ready to host US-Iran talks’: Can latest peace push work?
article.summary:
Islamabad, Pakistan – The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has said Pakistan is ready to host talks between the United States and Iran amid US President Donald Trump’s claims of ongoing negotiations between Washington and Tehran.
“If the parties desire, Islamabad is always willing to host talks,” Foreign Ministry spokesperson Tahir Andrabi told Al Jazeera on Tuesday.
One involves Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, US envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner.
AdvertisementStill, some facts are confirmed: Pakistani army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir spoke to President Trump on Sunday.
Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif called Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian a day later.

Brands must now clear strict national security checks to launch new router models in the US.
The US is cracking down on foreign-made Wi-Fi routers.
The body concluded that foreign-made routers pose “unacceptable risks” to US infrastructure and citizens.
This conditional approval is granted if Wi-Fi router manufacturers can prove to the US authorities that their devices do not pose security risks.

article.title: Is Your Wi-Fi Router Safe From the FCC Ban? Nearly Every Major Brand Is Impacted
article.summary:

Because any router that’s available now has already gotten FCC authorization, there’s no need to rush out and buy a new router.

article.title: Will the Houthis join Iran in war against Israel and the US?
article.summary:
The Yemeni armed group says all options are on the table.
As the US-Israeli war against Iran drags on, Yemen’s Ansar Allah, or the Houthis, have stayed out of the conflict.
They have said they consider themselves directly concerned and could take a position alongside Iran.
The armed group has attacked Israel and shipping in the Red Sea in recent years.
So, will the Houthis join the war?

article.title: No quick end to conflict, global markets to stay on edge: Adrian Mowat
article.summary:
SynopsisGlobal markets are experiencing volatility due to shifting geopolitical developments, particularly concerning the US and Iran.
Oil prices have surged, reflecting supply disruption fears, while India faces risks from oil costs and AI's impact on its IT sector.
Bond markets are also adjusting to potential US rate hikes.

article.title: Trump can declare victory in Iran – and he should
article.summary:
Instead of drifting into another conventional decade-long war, Trump chose a simple formula: hit hard, hit fast, and make it clear that the US will not capitulate to threats.
He will say it directly: America struck, America won, and America did so without engaging in another endless war.
Declaring victory at the moment of peak strength sends a powerful signal — not just to Iran, but to the entire world.
Critics on the American left will predictably label any Trump victory “premature” and his methods “illegal” and reckless.
Trump’s Iran victory speech will not be lofty and replete with platitudes.

article.title: Your wireless router is now banned from sale in the US
article.summary:
Almost every new wireless router for use in US homes is now banned from sale within the country under a new Federal Communications Commission (FCC) ruling.
A huge dealThis is a huge deal because almost every wireless router used in US homes is made outside the US.
This includes the routers supplied as standard by internet service providers as well as models people buy to get better features.
The ban is so extensive because of the way a foreign-made router is defined in the ruling.
What it means for youIt’s important to stress that the ban applies to the importation and sale rather than the use of new foreign-made routers.

article.title: FCC bans import of new consumer routers not made in the US over security threat — agency says foreign-made devices pose ‘unacceptable risk’ to US persons
article.summary:
Still, this did not deter the Department of Commerce from investigating TP-Link over its close ties to China.
This isn’t the first time that the FCC issued a directive that had a blanket effect on a specific type of device.
Many of the best Wi-Fi routers on sale in 2026 are from foreign companies like TP-Link.
The FCC's new measure could, in theory, preclude future new products from these companies from coming to the States.
